opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My oncologist's receptionist just told me my referral is expired and they will cancel my chemotherapy tomorrow morning unless I bring a new one. I am desperate.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: I need a new, valid specialist referral letter before my chemotherapy appointment at 8 o'clock tomorrow morning, or they said they'll cancel it.

symptom_narrative.onset: this evening, right after a phone call from the cancer clinic's receptionist
symptom_narrative.duration: the last few hours, since that phone call
symptom_narrative.character: overwhelming panic and dread about missing tomorrow's chemotherapy; heart pounding, shaky, can't settle
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst worry I've felt in a long time — I'm desperate and can't think straight
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: it's all in my chest and my head — my heart is racing
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant since the phone call, worse whenever I think about the appointment being cancelled
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing yet — I just need the referral sorted out
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: thinking about my treatment being stopped
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: my heart feels like it's racing; otherwise I have no fever, no pain, no vomiting, and I've been coping with my chemo cycles fine
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I've never panicked like this before, but I've also never had my paperwork lapse before
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I can't relax or sleep tonight; otherwise I manage on my own at home and get to my appointments myself

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: bowel (colon) cancer that my cancer specialist is treating
past_medical_history.how_managed: ongoing chemotherapy under my oncologist, with regular clinic reviews

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: capecitabine chemotherapy tablets (started by my cancer specialist)

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: elderly, lives independently and manages her own care; attends the oncology clinic regularly; alone at home this evening and very distressed about the deadline

objective_data_offered.value: 36.5 on my home thermometer just now
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single self-taken reading on a home thermometer; does not exclude a low blood count

objective_data_offered.value: my heart feels like it's pounding and racing
I'm shaky and panicky, but I don't actually feel physically unwell — no fever, no pain, no being sick
